syllableStructure: CVC (consonant-vowel-consonant), only allowing consonant clusters across syllable boundaries.
phonologicalEvolution: Classification based on shared changes (e.g. */r/ > [j] in western group, [r] retained in K‚Äôichean, [t É] or [t] in Mamean). Palatalized plosives [t ≤ ľ], [t ≤] reflected as [t], [t ľ], [ts], [ts ľ], [t É], [t É ľ] depending on branch. *[Ňč] reflected as [x], [n], [h], or conserved as [Ňč].
diphthongs: Monophthongal vowels; V-V sequences realized with hiatus-breaking glottal stop or glide. Some Quichean languages developed diphthongs from historical long vowels.

grammar.morphology: Agglutinating, polysynthetic. Verbs marked for aspect/tense, subject/object person, plurality. Nouns marked for number and person of possessor.
grammar.wordOrder: Basic VOS or VSO, some SVO. Flexible in some languages.
grammar.numeralClassifiers: Numeral classifiers required in many languages, specifying class of items (shape, animacy, etc.).
grammar.possession: Possessed nouns marked for person/number of possessor. Alienable/inalienable distinction in some languages.
grammar.relationalNouns: Location/relations expressed with relational nouns, often derived from body parts.
grammar.alignment: Ergative alignment: Set A (agent/possessor), Set B (subject of intransitive/object of transitive).
grammar.verbStructure: Aspect/mood/tense + Set A prefix + Set B prefix + root + aspect/mood/voice + plural.
grammar.voices: Passive, antipassive, mediopassive, incorporational, instrumental, referential.
grammar.statives: Predicative words expressing quality/state, inflected for person but not tense/aspect.
grammar.positionals: Roots forming stative adjectives/verbs related to position/shape (250‚Äď500 roots).
grammar.wordFormation: Compounding, derivational affixes, metaphorical use of body part roots for locatives.

references: Campbell, Lyle & Kaufman, Terrence (2000). Historical Linguistics and Mayan Languages.

palatoAlveolarRetroflex.tone: In tonal languages (primarily Yucatec), a high tone is indicated with an accent, as with '√°' or '√©e'.
notes: En linguistique, on utilise 'Mayan' pour les langues, 'Maya' dans d'autres domaines.
Achi ľ est une variante de K ľiche ľ selon le gouvernement guat√©malt√®que.
